title: 2026 Jacobian counterexample reproduction
statement: Reproduce the announced polynomial map in three variables, verify that its Jacobian determinant is the nonzero constant -2, and verify that three distinct rational points have the same image.
source_name: MathOverflow verification thread and cited announcement
verifiability: Exact symbolic identity and three rational witness evaluations
rationale: A small, decisive, independently reproducible calibration of the entire evidence pipeline. This is prior work, not a Proof Factory discovery.

title: Erdős–Straus conjecture
statement: For every integer n > 2, do there exist distinct integers 1 ≤ x < y < z such that 4/n = 1/x + 1/y + 1/z?
verifiability: A counterexample is finite; proposed parametric families and congruence covers can be checked exactly; the statement has a third-party Lean formalization.
rationale: The initial famous lane: genuinely hard, widely known, precisely stated, computationally grounded, and decomposable into congruence and parametric subproblems.

title: A divisor-function record beyond 24
statement: Let τ(n) count the positive divisors of n. Is there some n > 24 such that max over m < n of (m + τ(m)) is at most n + 2?
verifiability: A positive answer is one explicit integer plus a bounded exact divisor-count check.
rationale: The first genuine discovery target: a short statement with a compact deterministic witness and a natural optimized-search harness.

title: Prime reciprocal product sets
statement: Are there finite sets of primes P and Q such that 1 = (sum over p in P of 1/p)(sum over q in Q of 1/q)?
verifiability: A positive answer is a pair of finite prime sets checked by exact rational arithmetic.
rationale: Finite certificates are decisive, though the known lower bound of 60 total primes makes search engineering nontrivial.

title: Johnson graph coloring witness
statement: Does some k > 2 admit a coloring of the k-subsets of {1,…,2k} with k+1 colors such that every (k+1)-subset sees all k+1 colors?
verifiability: A coloring is a finite certificate; SAT/CP-SAT encodings can be independently checked.
rationale: A clean finite combinatorics search with reusable SAT certificates; known false for 3 ≤ k ≤ 8 narrows the first unknown cases.

title: Distinct odd covering system
statement: Does there exist a distinct covering system all of whose moduli are odd?
verifiability: An explicit finite covering system can be checked over one least-common-multiple period.
rationale: A finite witness would settle the existence direction, but structural lower bounds make it a later discovery-lane target.

title: Erdős problem #1041
statement: Let $f(z)=\prod_{i=1}^n(z-z_i)\in \mathbb{C}[z]$ with $\lvert z_i\rvert < 1$ for all $i$. Must there always exist a path of length less than $2$ in\[\{z: \lvert f(z)\rvert < 1\}\]which connects two of the roots of $f$?
rationale: Added from the versioned Erdős Problems community database to keep the discovery frontier broad. The first pass must validate the exact statement, status, literature, and a concrete verification contract.
verifiability: Official database status is falsifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.

title: Erdős problem #1082
statement: Let $A\subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of $n$ points with no three on a line. Does $A$ determine at least $\lfloor n/2\rfloor$ distinct distances? In fact, must there exist a single point from which there are at least $\lfloor n/2\rfloor$ distinct distances?
rationale: Added from the versioned Erdős Problems community database to keep the discovery frontier broad. The first pass must validate the exact statement, status, literature, and a concrete verification contract.
verifiability: Official database status is falsifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.

title: Erdős problem #23
statement: Can every triangle-free graph on $5n$ vertices be made bipartite by deleting at most $n^2$ edges?

title: Erdős problem #287
statement: Let $k\geq 2$. Is it true that, for any distinct integers $1<n_1<\cdots <n_k$ such that\[1=\frac{1}{n_1}+\cdots+\frac{1}{n_k}\]we must have $\max(n_{i+1}-n_i)\geq 3$?
rationale: Added from the versioned Erdős Problems community database to keep the discovery frontier broad. The first pass must validate the exact statement, status, literature, and a concrete verification contract.
verifiability: Official database status is falsifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.

title: Erdős problem #364
statement: Are there any triples of consecutive positive integers all of which are powerful (i.e. if $p\mid n$ then $p^2\mid n$)?
rationale: Added from the versioned Erdős Problems community database to keep the discovery frontier broad. The first pass must validate the exact statement, status, literature, and a concrete verification contract.

title: Erdős problem #366
statement: Are there any $2$-full $n$ such that $n+1$ is $3$-full? That is, if $p\mid n$ then $p^2\mid n$ and if $p\mid n+1$ then $p^3\mid n+1$.
rationale: Added from the versioned Erdős Problems community database to keep the discovery frontier broad. The first pass must validate the exact statement, status, literature, and a concrete verification contract.
verifiability: Official database status is verifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.

title: Erdős problem #375
statement: Is it true that for any $n,k\geq 1$, if $n+1,\ldots,n+k$ are all composite then there are distinct primes $p_1,\ldots,p_k$ such that $p_i\mid n+i$ for $1\leq i\leq k$?
rationale: Added from the versioned Erdős Problems community database to keep the discovery frontier broad. The first pass must validate the exact statement, status, literature, and a concrete verification contract.
